title: Argo CD Prune Disabled with Auto-Sync Enabled (Policy Drift)
dataSources: Application spec JSON with `syncPolicy.automated` and prune flags (`sourcetype=argocd:application`)
app: Argo CD API exporter or Kubernetes watch → HEC
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="argocd:application"
| search automated="true" OR sync_policy_automated="true"
| search prune="false" OR sync_policy_prune="false" OR "prune\":false"
| table _time, name, namespace, project, revision, sync_policy_automated
| sort -_time
description: Auto-sync without prune accumulates orphaned resources and hides drift.
value: Flags risky GitOps posture where deleted manifests never remove cluster objects.
implementation: Ingest full application spec nightly; alert in staging clusters too. Pair with OPA policy audits if available.
visualization: Table (non-pruning automated apps), Pie chart (prune on vs off).

references.title: Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring App for Argo CD (GitHub)

knownFalsePositives: Drift from manual console fixes during incidents, automated tool changes, or planned config updates outside IaC.
grandmaExplanation: We watch GitOps sync and Argo signals so clusters stay aligned with the desired configuration in Git.
